sanskrit: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः। प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम्॥
transliteration: tasmād om ity udāhṛtya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ / pravartante vidhāna-uktāḥ satataṃ brahma-vādinām
literal_meaning: Therefore, having uttered ‘Om,’ sacrifices, charity and penance are always undertaken by those who speak of Brahman in accordance with scriptural ordinance.
contextual_meaning: All meaningful action is preceded by a symbolic cue that links the act to a larger, sacred narrative; the syllable Om functions as a cognitive anchor that shifts the mind from scattered self-referential mode to a transpersonal, value-consistent mode.
psychological_state: A deliberately primed state of self-transcendence in which the prefrontal ‘executive’ is briefly overridden by a symbolic mantra, reducing egoic noise before engaging in prosocial or ascetic behavior.
trigger_scenario: You pause before hitting ‘send’ on a generous donation, whisper a one-word intention like ‘service,’ and feel the shift from transactional to meaningful giving.
anchor_text: Begin every deed with Om to align mind, word, and sacred purpose.
